text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY REPORT
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Margaret Chen
MRN: [REDACTED]
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [REDACTED]
DATE OF REPORT: [REDACTED]
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [REDACTED], MD — Hematology/Oncology / Bone Marrow Transplant
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED], MD — Hematopathology
SPECIMEN ACCESSION: [REDACTED]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
  1A: Bone marrow core biopsy, right posterior iliac crest
  1B: Bone marrow aspirate clot section, right posterior iliac crest
  1C: Bone marrow aspirate smears (6 slides submitted)
  1D: Fresh bone marrow aspirate — submitted for flow cytometry MRD panel (see addendum)
  1E: Fresh bone marrow aspirate — submitted for cytogenetics / chimerism studies (see addendum)
  1F: Fresh bone marrow aspirate — submitted for molecular studies / PCR (see addendum)

CLINICAL HISTORY:
The patient is a 49-year-old woman with a history of de novo acute myeloid leukemia (AML), NOS, diagnosed approximately 12 months prior (at age 48). At initial diagnosis, bone marrow showed 72% myeloblasts with Auer rods; immunophenotype CD34+/CD117+/CD13+/CD33+/MPO+/HLA-DR+. Conventional karyotype 46,XX (normal karyotype). FLT3-ITD positive with high allelic ratio (0.68). Co-mutations included DNMT3A p.R882H, RUNX1 p.S291fs, TET2 p.Q916*, WT1 p.R462Q, and PTPN11 p.E76K. ELN 2022 risk: Adverse.

The patient received induction chemotherapy with cytarabine/daunorubicin (7+3) plus midostaurin (RATIFY protocol) and achieved morphologic complete remission (CR) with MRD negativity by flow cytometry after a single induction cycle (Day +28 biopsy). She proceeded directly to myeloablative allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (alloHSCT) from a 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor (MUD), male, age 32, CMV seropositive. Conditioning: fludarabine/busulfan (FluBu4). GVHD prophylaxis: tacrolimus/Trexall. Post-transplant course complicated by grade 3 mucositis and transient tacrolimus toxicity; mild chronic GVHD (skin and GI, NIH global: mild), successfully managed with topical corticosteroids and oral budesonide. Tacrolimus successfully tapered and discontinued at approximately 11 months post-transplant without GVHD flare. Post-transplant Day +30, +180 biopsies: both showed morphologic CR, full donor chimerism, MRD negativity.

This biopsy is being performed as routine 12-month post-alloHSCT surveillance. The patient is clinically well with no symptoms to suggest relapse or GVHD.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen 1A (Core Biopsy): Received in 10% buffered formalin is a single bone marrow core biopsy measuring 1.6 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter. The specimen is entirely submitted. Decalcification performed with formic acid per standard protocol. Sections prepared at multiple levels and stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E), periodic acid-Schiff (PAS), reticulin (silver stain), and iron (Prussian blue).

Specimen 1B (Clot Section): Received in 10% buffered formalin is a bone marrow aspirate clot consisting of a fragmented red-tan tissue aggregate measuring 0.8 x 0.5 x 0.3 cm in aggregate. Entirely submitted in one cassette. Stained with H&E and PAS.

Specimen 1C (Aspirate Smears): Six unstained and six Wright-Giemsa-stained smears submitted. Smears are of adequate cellularity. A 500-cell differential count is performed on the best quality smear.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Bone Marrow Biopsy (Core) — H&E:
The core biopsy is adequate for evaluation. The marrow space demonstrates normocellular marrow with an estimated cellularity of 40-50%, appropriate for patient age (49 years). The bony trabeculae are intact with no evidence of osteoblastic or osteoclastic rimming beyond physiologic limits. Fat spaces are adequate and evenly distributed.

Hematopoiesis is trilineage with orderly maturation observed in all three lineages:

Myeloid series: Myeloid precursors are present at all stages of maturation from myeloblasts to mature segmented neutrophils. Myeloblasts are not conspicuously increased and are not forming clusters or sheets. Auer rods are NOT identified in this specimen. Maturing granulocytes, including promyelocytes, myelocytes, metamyelocytes, and bands, are present in normal proportions.

Erythroid series: Erythroid precursors are present in normal numbers as scattered clusters of normoblastic cells showing orderly maturation from proerythroblasts through orthochromatic normoblasts. No dysplastic features (megaloblastoid change, nuclear bridging, cytoplasmic vacuolization) are identified.

Megakaryocytic series: Megakaryocytes are present in adequate numbers (estimated 3-5 per high-power field at the core). Megakaryocytes appear mature with appropriate lobulation and abundant cytoplasm. No micromegakaryocytes, bare megakaryocyte nuclei, or significant clustering noted.

No sheets, aggregates, or clusters of blasts are identified. No abnormal localization of immature precursors (ALIP) is present. No paratrabecular lymphoid aggregates or interstitial infiltrates suggestive of lymphoma or other hematologic malignancy are identified.

Lymphoid cells are present in normal proportions as small mature lymphocytes without cytologic atypia.

Plasma cells are present in low numbers (<1%), appearing mature and non-atypical.

Bone Marrow Aspirate Smears — Wright-Giemsa:
500-cell differential count:
  Myeloblasts:         1.0%
  Promyelocytes:       3.2%
  Myelocytes:          10.4%
  Metamyelocytes:      8.8%
  Bands:               12.6%
  Segmented neutrophils: 18.4%
  Eosinophils:         2.4%
  Basophils:           0.4%
  Monocytes:           2.8%
  Lymphocytes:         14.2%
  Plasma cells:        0.6%
  Erythroid precursors: 25.2%
  ----------------------------------------
  Myeloid:Erythroid (M:E) ratio: approximately 3.0:1

Blast percentage: 1.0% — WITHIN NORMAL LIMITS (<5% consistent with complete remission criteria).

No Auer rods identified. No dysplastic neutrophils (hypogranulation, Pelger-Huet anomaly), no ring sideroblasts identified on iron stain. Erythroid precursors appear normoblastic throughout all stages. No circulating or aspirate blasts with the immunophenotypic characteristics of the original AML clone are identified morphologically.

Note: Aspirate smear quality is good. Spicules are present. Cellularity of the aspirate is consistent with the core biopsy.

Special Stains:
- PAS stain: Erythroid precursors show appropriate block and diffuse positivity consistent with normal erythroid differentiation. No abnormal PAS-positive blast populations identified.
- Reticulin stain (MF grading per WHO 2022): MF-0. Normal reticulin fiber density. No significant increase in reticulin fibrosis. No collagen fibrosis.
- Iron stain (Prussian blue): Storage iron (hemosiderin) is present in macrophages and is adequate (2+ on scale of 0-4+). Sideroblasts present in appropriate proportion. No ring sideroblasts identified (0 of 30 erythroid precursors counted).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

Flow Cytometry — MRD Assessment (10-color panel; Specimen 1D):
PERFORMED. See separate flow cytometry report (Accession [REDACTED]).
PRELIMINARY RESULT: No immunophenotypically aberrant blast population detected.
The following gates were analyzed: CD45 vs. side scatter analysis; blast gate defined as CD45-dim events. Immunophenotypic analysis of blast gate using antibody panel including CD34, CD117, CD33, CD13, CD7, CD19, CD56, MPO, HLA-DR, CD11b, CD64, CD14, CD38, CD123, TdT.
FINAL RESULT (incorporated at sign-out): MRD NEGATIVE — No immunophenotypically aberrant blast population detected above the limit of detection (0.01%). Total events acquired: 462,000. The blast gate contained <0.005% of all events, and no immunophenotypic aberrancy consistent with the known AML immunophenotype (CD34+/CD117+/CD33+/CD13+/MPO+/HLA-DR+/CD7-dim) was identified.

Cytogenetics / Chimerism Studies (Specimen 1E):
PERFORMED. See separate cytogenetics and chimerism report (Accession [REDACTED]).
Conventional karyotype: Specimen sent; result PENDING at time of preliminary sign-out. Expected turnaround: 10-14 days.
Short Tandem Repeat (STR) Chimerism Analysis: PERFORMED.
FINAL RESULT (incorporated at sign-out): TOTAL CHIMERISM 99.8% DONOR (patient/donor sex mismatch not applicable; analysis by 16-locus STR panel). CD3+ T-cell fraction: 97.2% donor. CD33+ myeloid fraction: 100% donor (no recipient-derived cells detected in the myeloid compartment above the limit of detection of 0.5%). These results are consistent with full donor engraftment and complete donor chimerism.

Molecular Studies (Specimen 1F):
PERFORMED. See separate molecular pathology report (Accession [REDACTED]).
  1. FLT3-ITD by quantitative PCR (sensitivity 0.001%): PENDING — RESULT EXPECTED WITHIN 5 BUSINESS DAYS. [NOTE: Prior Day +180 result was not detected.]
  2. DNMT3A p.R882H by droplet digital PCR (ddPCR) (sensitivity <0.01%): PENDING — RESULT EXPECTED WITHIN 5 BUSINESS DAYS.
  3. RUNX1 p.S291fs*16 by deep targeted NGS sequencing (sensitivity <0.1%): PENDING — RESULT EXPECTED WITHIN 7 BUSINESS DAYS.
  [These molecular studies will be reported as an addendum upon result availability.]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS:

1. NORMOCELLULAR BONE MARROW WITH NORMAL TRILINEAGE HEMATOPOIESIS — no morphologic evidence of residual or recurrent acute myeloid leukemia (AML).

2. MYELOBLASTS: 1% of nucleated cells (within normal limits, satisfying morphologic complete remission [CR] criteria of <5% blasts).

3. NO AUER RODS identified.

4. MRD by 10-color flow cytometry: NEGATIVE — no immunophenotypically aberrant blast population detected (limit of detection 0.01%).

5. DONOR CHIMERISM by STR analysis: 99.8% total donor chimerism; 100% donor in CD33+ myeloid fraction; 97.2% donor in CD3+ T-cell fraction — FULL DONOR CHIMERISM CONFIRMED.

6. RETICULIN FIBROSIS: MF-0 — no increase in reticulin fibrosis.

7. CONVENTIONAL KARYOTYPE and MOLECULAR STUDIES (FLT3-ITD, DNMT3A, RUNX1): PENDING — results to follow in addendum.

8. NO EVIDENCE OF GRAFT-VERSUS-HOST DISEASE within the bone marrow compartment (no significant lymphocytic infiltrate, no histiocytic aggregates, no aplasia or dyshematopoiesis attributable to GVHD).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ASSESSMENT / SUMMARY:

Bone marrow biopsy and aspirate performed for routine 12-month post-allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (alloHSCT) surveillance in a 49-year-old woman with history of de novo FLT3-ITD positive AML (ELN Adverse risk).

Findings demonstrate SUSTAINED MORPHOLOGIC COMPLETE REMISSION with normal trilineage hematopoiesis at 40-50% cellularity. Blast count is 1% by 500-cell differential count, well within the <5% threshold for morphologic CR. No Auer rods, no dysplastic features, and no immunophenotypically aberrant blast population are identified.

Full donor chimerism is confirmed by STR analysis (99.8% total; 100% myeloid). MRD by 10-color flow cytometry is NEGATIVE at the 0.01% threshold, consistent with ongoing complete molecular remission as demonstrated on the Day +30, +180, and prior assessments.

Molecular MRD studies (FLT3-ITD qPCR, DNMT3A ddPCR, RUNX1 deep sequencing) are pending and will be reported as an addendum. Conventional karyotype is also pending.

Overall impression: Durable complete remission with full donor chimerism and MRD negativity at 12 months post-alloHSCT. No morphologic or immunophenotypic evidence of AML recurrence.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ADDENDUM (issued upon receipt of pending studies):

Conventional Karyotype (received, result finalized):
46,XY [20/20 metaphases analyzed] — donor male karyotype confirmed. No clonal cytogenetic abnormalities detected. Consistent with complete donor engraftment. No residual recipient (female, 46,XX) metaphases identified.

FLT3-ITD by quantitative PCR: NOT DETECTED (sensitivity 0.001%). No FLT3-ITD signal above background. Consistent with molecular remission.

DNMT3A p.R882H by ddPCR: NOT DETECTED (sensitivity <0.01%). No mutant allele droplets identified above assay threshold.

RUNX1 p.S291fs*16 by deep targeted NGS: NOT DETECTED (sensitivity <0.1%; 85,000x sequencing depth at the target locus). No pathogenic frameshift variant identified.

ADDENDUM SUMMARY: All pending molecular studies are now resulted. FLT3-ITD, DNMT3A p.R882H, and RUNX1 p.S291fs are NOT DETECTED at the respective limits of detection. Combined with MRD negativity by flow cytometry and full donor chimerism, findings confirm SUSTAINED COMPLETE MOLECULAR REMISSION at 12 months post-allogeneic SCT.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SIGNED BY: [REDACTED], MD
Division of Hematopathology
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

Electronically signed and verified.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
